name: Windows Spooler Service Suspicious Binary Load
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: FPT.EagleEye, Thomas Patzke (improvements)
SIGMA Title: Windows Spooler Service Suspicious Binary Load
SIGMA Description: Detect DLL Load from Spooler Service backup folder

False positives:
- Loading of legitimate driver

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=12 AND (LOWER("Process Path") LIKE '%\spoolsv.exe' OR LOWER("Process Name") LIKE '%\spoolsv.exe') AND (LOWER(CONCAT('file directory', '/', filename)) LIKE '%\windows\system32\spool\drivers\x64\3\%' OR LOWER(CONCAT('file directory', '/', filename)) LIKE '%\windows\system32\spool\drivers\x64\4\%') AND LOWER(CONCAT('file directory', '/', filename)) LIKE '%.dll' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Windows Spooler Service Suspicious Binary Load on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when the event matches devicetype=12 AND (LOWER("Process Path") LIKE '%\spoolsv.exe' OR LOWER("Process Name") LIKE '%\spoolsv.exe') AND (LOWER(CONCAT('file directory', '/', filename)) LIKE '%\windows\system32\spool\drivers\x64\3\%' OR LOWER(CONCAT('file directory', '/', filename)) LIKE '%\windows\system32\spool\drivers\x64\4\%') AND LOWER(CONCAT('file directory', '/', filename)) LIKE '%.dll' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Windows Spooler Service Suspicious Binary Load

Event description: Converted from SIGMA Rule. Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Event Name
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
